ResourceHeader.Description: NASA utilizes Barentsburg as a ground observation site for tracking Earth’s magnetic field and magnetospheric dynamics. Located in Svalbard, Norway, the settlement hosts a vector magnetometer that feeds data into NASA's Space Physics Data Facility to help researchers monitor space weather.
ResourceHeader.Acknowledgement: As defined in Rasson J.L., (2001). The status of world-wide network of magnetic observatories, their location and instrumentation, Contributions to Geophysics & Geodesy, 31, 1, pp 427-453

InformationURL.Name: IAGA Guides and Standards
InformationURL.Description: Overview of IAGA Guides and Standards which obtained within its Divisions and Working Groups. Information about observatories is maintained by the "Geomagntic Observatories" (V-OBS) working group.
